Caption: TASEC-Lab during the pre-launch activities (a) and integration of the TASEC-Lab EPS (b).
TASEC-Lab power subsystem simplified electrical circuit.
Estimated power consumption, P , on the ＋5 V and ＋12 V lines during the TASEC-Lab first mission (July 2021).
Battery equivalent circuit during a discharging process at constant rate.

Typical Li-ion battery discharging curve. Battery output voltage, V , is plotted as a function of the discharged energy, ϕ , for three discharging currents such I 1 > I 2 > I 3 . The three areas in which the battery shows different behavior (that is, different evolution of the voltage level) are indicated in the graph.

Evolution of the battery output voltage, V , as a function of the discharged energy, ϕ , for different discharging current rates. Experimental results are plotted with solid lines and the fitting curves with dashed lines. The results of the linear model described in [19,27] and the obtained with the new model from the present research are plotted in (a) and (b), respectively.
